revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=爾波神社| 所在地=愛知県一宮市丹羽字宮浦1410| 位置=| 祭神=[[神八井耳命]]| 社格=式内社・旧郷社| 創建=不明| }}
'''若栗神社'''（わぐりじんじゃ）は、[[愛知県]][[一宮市]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は郷社。
[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]の[[尾張国]][[丹羽郡]]「尓波神社」である。丹羽郡の総社と伝えられ、[[丹羽氏|丹羽臣]]に関係が深い。
== 概況 == *創建時期は不明。一説では[[701年]]（[[大宝]]元年）創建という。この地域に勢力があった尾張丹羽臣が、祖先の神八井耳命を祀ったのが始まりという。 *創建当初の名称は尓波神社。[[鎌倉時代]]以降は「丹羽天神」「丹羽大神」「爾波天神」と呼ばれていた。 *[[1537年]]（[[天文 (元号)|天文]]6年）に戦災で社殿焼失する。再建されるが、[[1666年]]（[[寛文]]6年）に洪水で破損。現在地に移転する。このさい、[[臨済宗]]の寺院（恵日山東光寺）の境内に再建され、明神社に改称する。 *[[1870年]]（明治3年）、爾波神社に改称され、[[1872年]]（明治5年）に郷社になる。 *[[1945年]]（昭和20年）7月28日、[[太平洋戦争]]の[[空襲]]（[[一宮空襲]]）で社殿は全て焼失する。現在の社殿は[[1948年]]（昭和23年）の再建である。
== 祭神 == *[[神八井耳命]]
== 所在地 == *愛知県一宮市丹羽字宮浦1410
== 交通機関 == *[[名鉄バス]]「丹羽」バス停下車、徒歩10分 **[[名古屋鉄道]][[名鉄名古屋本線|名古屋本線]]・[[名鉄尾西線|尾西線]][[名鉄一宮駅]]、[[東海道本線]][[尾張一宮駅]]（[[名鉄一宮駅バスターミナル]]3番のりば）より「宮田本郷（丹羽経由）」行き
{{DEFAULTSORT:にわしんしや}} [[Category:愛知県の神社]] [[Category:一宮市]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 名称=爾波神社| 所在地=愛知県一宮市丹羽字宮浦1410| 位置=| 祭神=[[神八井耳命]]| 社格=式内社・旧郷社| 創建=不明| }}

== 祭神 == *[[神八井耳命]]
== 所在地 == *愛知県一宮市丹羽字宮浦1410
== 交通機関 == *[[名鉄バス]]「丹羽」バス停下車、徒歩10分 **[[名古屋鉄道]][[名鉄名古屋本線|名古屋本線]]・[[名鉄尾西線|尾西線]][[名鉄一宮駅]]、[[東海道本線]][[尾張一宮駅]]（[[名鉄一宮駅バスターミナル]]3番のりば）より「宮田本郷（丹羽経由）」行き

{{出典の明記|date=2014年10月27日 (月) 12:50 (UTC)}} {{画像提供依頼|date=2014年10月27日 (月) 12:50 (UTC)|cat=一宮市|外観}} {{神社| 名称=爾波神社| 所在地=愛知県一宮市丹羽字宮浦1410| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|19|26.83|N|136|49|10.50|E|scale:30000}}| 祭神=[[神八井耳命]]| 社格=式内社・旧郷社| 創建=不明| }}
'''爾波神社'''（にわじんじゃ）は、[[愛知県]][[一宮市]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[氏子調#行政単位と郷社|郷社]]。
[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]の[[尾張国]][[丹羽郡]]「尓波神社」である。丹羽郡の総社と伝えられ、[[丹羽氏|丹羽臣]]に関係が深い。
== 概況 == * 創建時期は不明。一説では[[701年]]（[[大宝 (日本)|大宝]]元年）創建という。この地域に勢力があった尾張丹羽臣が、祖先の神八井耳命を祀ったのが始まりという。 * 創建当初の名称は尓波神社。[[鎌倉時代]]以降は「丹羽天神」「丹羽大神」「爾波天神」と呼ばれていた。 * [[1537年]]（[[天文 (元号)|天文]]6年）に戦災で社殿焼失する。再建されるが、[[1666年]]（[[寛文]]6年）に洪水で破損。現在地に移転する。このさい、[[臨済宗]]の寺院（恵日山東光寺）の境内に再建され、明神社に改称する。 * [[1870年]]（明治3年）、爾波神社に改称され、[[1872年]]（明治5年）に郷社になる。 * [[1945年]]（昭和20年）7月28日、[[太平洋戦争]]の[[空襲]]（[[一宮空襲]]）で社殿は全て焼失する。現在の社殿は[[1948年]]（昭和23年）の再建である。
== 祭神 == * [[神八井耳命]]
== 所在地 == * 愛知県一宮市丹羽字宮浦1410
== 交通機関 == * [[名鉄バス]]「丹羽」バス停下車、徒歩10分 ** [[名古屋鉄道]][[名鉄名古屋本線|名古屋本線]]・[[名鉄尾西線|尾西線]][[名鉄一宮駅]]、[[東海道本線]][[尾張一宮駅]]（[[名鉄一宮駅バスターミナル]]3番のりば）より「宮田本郷（丹羽経由）」行き
{{DEFAULTSORT:にわしんしや}} [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧郷社]] {{shinto-stub}}

== 祭神 == * [[神八井耳命]]
== 交通機関 == * [[名鉄バス]]「丹羽」バス停下車、徒歩10分 ** [[名古屋鉄道]][[名鉄名古屋本線|名古屋本線]]・[[名鉄尾西線|尾西線]][[名鉄一宮駅]]、[[東海道本線]][[尾張一宮駅]]（[[名鉄一宮駅バスターミナル]]3番のりば）より「宮田本郷（丹羽経由）」行き

丹羽大神| |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''爾波神社'''（にわじんじゃ）は、[[愛知県]][[一宮市]][[丹羽 (一宮市)|丹羽]]{{small|字}}[[丹羽宮浦|宮浦]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[郷社]]。 [[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]の[[尾張国]][[丹羽郡]]「尓波神社」である。丹羽郡の[[総社]]と伝えられ、[[丹羽臣]]（[[丹羽氏]]）に関係が深い。 == 概況 == * 創建時期は不明。一説では[[701年]]（[[大宝 (日本)|大宝]]元年）創建という。この地域に勢力があった[[尾張丹羽臣]]が、祖先の神八井耳命を祀ったのが始まりという。 * 創建当初の名称は尓波神社。[[鎌倉時代]]以降は「丹羽天神」「丹羽大神」「爾波天神」と呼ばれていた。 * [[1537年]]（[[天文 (元号)|天文]]6年）に戦災で社殿焼失する。再建されるが、[[1666年]]（[[寛文]]6年）に洪水で破損。現在地に移転する。このさい、[[臨済宗]]の寺院（[[恵日山東光寺|恵日山]][[東光寺 (一宮市)|東光寺]]）[https://yaokami.jp/1236649/ yaokami]の境内に再建され、'''[[明神]]社'''に改称する。 * [[1870年]]（明治3年）、'''爾波神社'''に改称され、[[1872年]]（明治5年）に[[郷社]]になる。 * [[1945年]]（昭和20年）7月28日、[[太平洋戦争]]の[[空襲]]（[[一宮空襲]]）で社殿は全て焼失する。現在の社殿は[[1948年]]（昭和23年）の再建である。 == 祭神 == * [[神八井耳命]] == 交通機関 == * [[名鉄バス]]「丹羽」バス停下車、徒歩10分 ** [[名古屋鉄道]][[名鉄名古屋本線|名古屋本線]]・[[名鉄尾西線|尾西線]][[名鉄一宮駅]]、[[東海道本線]][[尾張一宮駅]]（[[名鉄一宮駅バスターミナル]]3番のりば）より「宮田本郷（丹羽経由）」行き == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にわしんしや}} [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:総社 (地域)]] [[Category:丹羽郡]] [[Category:丹羽氏]] [[Category:鎮守社]] [[Category:明神]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧郷社]] [[Category:多氏|社]] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=愛知県}}

{{出典の明記|date=2014年10月27日 (月) 12:50 (UTC)}} {{神社| 名称=爾波神社| 画像=[[File:Niwa-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|300px]]| 所在地=愛知県一宮市丹羽字宮浦1410| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|19|26.83|N|136|49|10.50|E|scale:30000}}| 祭神=[[神八井耳命]]| 社格=式内社・旧郷社| 創建=不明| }}
'''爾波神社'''（にわじんじゃ）は、[[愛知県]][[一宮市]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[郷社]]。
[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]の[[尾張国]][[丹羽郡]]「尓波神社」である。丹羽郡の総社と伝えられ、[[丹羽氏|丹羽臣]]に関係が深い。
== 概況 == * 創建時期は不明。一説では[[701年]]（[[大宝 (日本)|大宝]]元年）創建という。この地域に勢力があった尾張丹羽臣が、祖先の神八井耳命を祀ったのが始まりという。 * 創建当初の名称は尓波神社。[[鎌倉時代]]以降は「丹羽天神」「丹羽大神」「爾波天神」と呼ばれていた。 * [[1537年]]（[[天文 (元号)|天文]]6年）に戦災で社殿焼失する。再建されるが、[[1666年]]（[[寛文]]6年）に洪水で破損。現在地に移転する。このさい、[[臨済宗]]の寺院（恵日山東光寺）の境内に再建され、明神社に改称する。 * [[1870年]]（明治3年）、爾波神社に改称され、[[1872年]]（明治5年）に郷社になる。 * [[1945年]]（昭和20年）7月28日、[[太平洋戦争]]の[[空襲]]（[[一宮空襲]]）で社殿は全て焼失する。現在の社殿は[[1948年]]（昭和23年）の再建である。
== 祭神 == * [[神八井耳命]]
== 交通機関 == * [[名鉄バス]]「丹羽」バス停下車、徒歩10分 ** [[名古屋鉄道]][[名鉄名古屋本線|名古屋本線]]・[[名鉄尾西線|尾西線]][[名鉄一宮駅]]、[[東海道本線]][[尾張一宮駅]]（[[名鉄一宮駅バスターミナル]]3番のりば）より「宮田本郷（丹羽経由）」行き
{{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にわしんしや}} [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧郷社]]

[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]の[[尾張国]][[丹羽郡]]「尓波神社」である。丹羽郡の総社と伝えられ、[[丹羽氏|丹羽臣]]に関係が深い。
== 概況 == * 創建時期は不明。一説では[[701年]]（[[大宝 (日本)|大宝]]元年）創建という。この地域に勢力があった尾張丹羽臣が、祖先の神八井耳命を祀ったのが始まりという。 * 創建当初の名称は尓波神社。[[鎌倉時代]]以降は「丹羽天神」「丹羽大神」「爾波天神」と呼ばれていた。 * [[1537年]]（[[天文 (元号)|天文]]6年）に戦災で社殿焼失する。再建されるが、[[1666年]]（[[寛文]]6年）に洪水で破損。現在地に移転する。このさい、[[臨済宗]]の寺院（恵日山東光寺）の境内に再建され、明神社に改称する。 * [[1870年]]（明治3年）、爾波神社に改称され、[[1872年]]（明治5年）に郷社になる。 * [[1945年]]（昭和20年）7月28日、[[太平洋戦争]]の[[空襲]]（[[一宮空襲]]）で社殿は全て焼失する。現在の社殿は[[1948年]]（昭和23年）の再建である。

{{nihongo|'''Niwa Shrine'''|爾波神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Niwa Shrine'''|爾波神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Niwa Shrine'''|爾波神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Niwa Shrine'''|爾波神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Niwa Shrine'''|爾波神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Niwa Shrine'''|爾波神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Niwa Shrine'''|爾波神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Niwa Shrine'''|爾波神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Niwa Shrine'''|爾波神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Niwa Shrine'''|爾波神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Niwa Shrine'''|爾波神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Niwa Shrine'''|爾波神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Niwa Shrine'''|爾波神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Niwa Shrine'''|爾波神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Niwa Shrine'''|爾波神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

revision.comment: 21 revisions imported
revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2014年10月27日 (月) 12:50 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称 = 爾波神社 |画像 = [[File:Niwa-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|250px]]
鳥居 |所在地 = [[愛知県]][[一宮市]][[丹羽 (一宮市)|丹羽]]字宮浦1410 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[神八井耳命]] |社格 = [[式内社]]・旧[[郷社]] |創建 = 不明 |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=爾波神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''爾波神社'''（にわじんじゃ）は、[[愛知県]][[一宮市]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[郷社]]。 [[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]の[[尾張国]][[丹羽郡]]「尓波神社」である。丹羽郡の総社と伝えられ、[[丹羽氏|丹羽臣]]に関係が深い。 == 概況 == * 創建時期は不明。一説では[[701年]]（[[大宝 (日本)|大宝]]元年）創建という。この地域に勢力があった尾張丹羽臣が、祖先の神八井耳命を祀ったのが始まりという。 * 創建当初の名称は尓波神社。[[鎌倉時代]]以降は「丹羽天神」「丹羽大神」「爾波天神」と呼ばれていた。 * [[1537年]]（[[天文 (元号)|天文]]6年）に戦災で社殿焼失する。再建されるが、[[1666年]]（[[寛文]]6年）に洪水で破損。現在地に移転する。このさい、[[臨済宗]]の寺院（恵日山東光寺）の境内に再建され、明神社に改称する。 * [[1870年]]（明治3年）、爾波神社に改称され、[[1872年]]（明治5年）に郷社になる。 * [[1945年]]（昭和20年）7月28日、[[太平洋戦争]]の[[空襲]]（[[一宮空襲]]）で社殿は全て焼失する。現在の社殿は[[1948年]]（昭和23年）の再建である。 == 祭神 == * [[神八井耳命]] == 交通機関 == * [[名鉄バス]]「丹羽」バス停下車、徒歩10分 ** [[名古屋鉄道]][[名鉄名古屋本線|名古屋本線]]・[[名鉄尾西線|尾西線]][[名鉄一宮駅]]、[[東海道本線]][[尾張一宮駅]]（[[名鉄一宮駅バスターミナル]]3番のりば）より「宮田本郷（丹羽経由）」行き == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にわしんしや}} [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧郷社]]

鳥居 |所在地 = [[愛知県]][[一宮市]][[丹羽 (一宮市)|丹羽]]字宮浦1410 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[神八井耳命]] |社格 = [[式内社]]・旧[[郷社]] |創建 = 不明 |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=爾波神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''爾波神社'''（にわじんじゃ）は、[[愛知県]][[一宮市]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[郷社]]。 [[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]の[[尾張国]][[丹羽郡]]「尓波神社」である。丹羽郡の総社と伝えられ、[[丹羽氏|丹羽臣]]に関係が深い。 == 概況 == * 創建時期は不明。一説では[[701年]]（[[大宝 (日本)|大宝]]元年）創建という。この地域に勢力があった尾張丹羽臣が、祖先の神八井耳命を祀ったのが始まりという。 * 創建当初の名称は尓波神社。[[鎌倉時代]]以降は「丹羽天神」「丹羽大神」「爾波天神」と呼ばれていた。 * [[1537年]]（[[天文 (元号)|天文]]6年）に戦災で社殿焼失する。再建されるが、[[1666年]]（[[寛文]]6年）に洪水で破損。現在地に移転する。このさい、[[臨済宗]]の寺院（恵日山東光寺）の境内に再建され、明神社に改称する。 * [[1870年]]（明治3年）、爾波神社に改称され、[[1872年]]（明治5年）に郷社になる。 * [[1945年]]（昭和20年）7月28日、[[太平洋戦争]]の[[空襲]]（[[一宮空襲]]）で社殿は全て焼失する。現在の社殿は[[1948年]]（昭和23年）の再建である。 == 祭神 == * [[神八井耳命]] == 交通機関 == * [[名鉄バス]]「丹羽」バス停下車、徒歩10分 ** [[名古屋鉄道]][[名鉄名古屋本線|名古屋本線]]・[[名鉄尾西線|尾西線]][[名鉄一宮駅]]、[[東海道本線]][[尾張一宮駅]]（[[名鉄一宮駅バスターミナル]]3番のりば）より「宮田本郷（丹羽経由）」行き == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にわしんしや}} [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧郷社]] ==End Japanese==

鳥居 |所在地 = [[愛知県]][[一宮市]][[丹羽 (一宮市)|丹羽]]字宮浦1410 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[神八井耳命]] |社格 = [[式内社]]・旧[[郷社]] |創建 = 不明 |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=爾波神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''爾波神社'''（にわじんじゃ）は、[[愛知県]][[一宮市]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[郷社]]。 [[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]の[[尾張国]][[丹羽郡]]「尓波神社」である。丹羽郡の総社と伝えられ、[[丹羽氏|丹羽臣]]に関係が深い。 == 概況 == * 創建時期は不明。一説では[[701年]]（[[大宝 (日本)|大宝]]元年）創建という。この地域に勢力があった尾張丹羽臣が、祖先の神八井耳命を祀ったのが始まりという。 * 創建当初の名称は尓波神社。[[鎌倉時代]]以降は「丹羽天神」「丹羽大神」「爾波天神」と呼ばれていた。 * [[1537年]]（[[天文 (元号)|天文]]6年）に戦災で社殿焼失する。再建されるが、[[1666年]]（[[寛文]]6年）に洪水で破損。現在地に移転する。このさい、[[臨済宗]]の寺院（恵日山東光寺）の境内に再建され、明神社に改称する。 * [[1870年]]（明治3年）、爾波神社に改称され、[[1872年]]（明治5年）に郷社になる。 * [[1945年]]（昭和20年）7月28日、[[太平洋戦争]]の[[空襲]]（[[一宮空襲]]）で社殿は全て焼失する。現在の社殿は[[1948年]]（昭和23年）の再建である。 == 祭神 == * [[神八井耳命]] == 交通機関 == * [[名鉄バス]]「丹羽」バス停下車、徒歩10分 ** [[名古屋鉄道]][[名鉄名古屋本線|名古屋本線]]・[[名鉄尾西線|尾西線]][[名鉄一宮駅]]、[[東海道本線]][[尾張一宮駅]]（[[名鉄一宮駅バスターミナル]]3番のりば）より「宮田本郷（丹羽経由）」行き == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧郷社]]

鳥居 |所在地 = {{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|一宮市|Q270810}}{{jalink|丹羽 (一宮市)|Q127432607|丹羽}}字宮浦1410 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|神八井耳命|Q11588756}} |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}・旧{{jalink|郷社}} |創建 = 不明 |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=爾波神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''爾波神社'''（にわじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|一宮市|Q270810}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|郷社}}。 {{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}の{{jalink|尾張国|Q514506}}{{jalink|丹羽郡|Q1347719}}「尓波神社」である。丹羽郡の総社と伝えられ、{{jalink|丹羽氏|Q2661652|丹羽臣}}に関係が深い。 == 概況 == * 創建時期は不明。一説では{{jalink|701年|Q23496}}（{{jalink|大宝 (日本)|Q1346269|大宝}}元年）創建という。この地域に勢力があった尾張丹羽臣が、祖先の神八井耳命を祀ったのが始まりという。 * 創建当初の名称は尓波神社。{{jalink|鎌倉時代|Q236205}}以降は「丹羽天神」「丹羽大神」「爾波天神」と呼ばれていた。 * {{jalink|1537年|Q6461}}（{{jalink|天文 (元号)|Q1192853|天文}}6年）に戦災で社殿焼失する。再建されるが、{{jalink|1666年|Q7585}}（{{jalink|寛文|Q1146228}}6年）に洪水で破損。現在地に移転する。このさい、{{jalink|臨済宗|Q639625}}の寺院（恵日山東光寺）の境内に再建され、明神社に改称する。 * {{jalink|1870年|Q7741}}（明治3年）、爾波神社に改称され、{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}（明治5年）に郷社になる。 * {{jalink|1945年|Q5240}}（昭和20年）7月28日、{{jalink|太平洋戦争|Q184425}}の{{jalink|空襲|Q2380335}}（{{jalink|一宮空襲|Q48744775}}）で社殿は全て焼失する。現在の社殿は{{jalink|1948年|Q5165}}（昭和23年）の再建である。 == 祭神 == * {{jalink|神八井耳命|Q11588756}} == 交通機関 == * {{jalink|名鉄バス|Q6810397}}「丹羽」バス停下車、徒歩10分 ** {{jalink|名古屋鉄道|Q30850}}{{jalink|名鉄名古屋本線|Q1137838|名古屋本線}}・{{jalink|名鉄尾西線|Q901005|尾西線}}{{jalink|名鉄一宮駅|Q2856589}}、{{jalink|東海道本線|Q1190152}}{{jalink|尾張一宮駅|Q871495}}（{{jalink|名鉄一宮駅バスターミナル}}3番のりば）より「宮田本郷（丹羽経由）」行き == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧郷社]]

link|Q11569496}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2014年10月27日 (月) 12:50 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称 = 爾波神社 |画像 = [[File:Niwa-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|250px]]
鳥居 |所在地 = {{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|一宮市|Q270810}}{{jalink|丹羽 (一宮市)|Q127432607|丹羽}}字宮浦1410 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|神八井耳命|Q11588756}} |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}・旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}} |創建 = 不明 |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=爾波神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''爾波神社'''（にわじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|一宮市|Q270810}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}。 {{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}の{{jalink|尾張国|Q514506}}{{jalink|丹羽郡|Q1347719}}「尓波神社」である。丹羽郡の総社と伝えられ、{{jalink|丹羽氏|Q2661652|丹羽臣}}に関係が深い。 === 概況 === * 創建時期は不明。一説では{{jalink|701年|Q23496}}（{{jalink|大宝 (日本)|Q1346269|大宝}}元年）創建という。この地域に勢力があった尾張丹羽臣が、祖先の神八井耳命を祀ったのが始まりという。 * 創建当初の名称は尓波神社。{{jalink|鎌倉時代|Q236205}}以降は「丹羽天神」「丹羽大神」「爾波天神」と呼ばれていた。 * {{jalink|1537年|Q6461}}（{{jalink|天文 (元号)|Q1192853|天文}}6年）に戦災で社殿焼失する。再建されるが、{{jalink|1666年|Q7585}}（{{jalink|寛文|Q1146228}}6年）に洪水で破損。現在地に移転する。このさい、{{jalink|臨済宗|Q639625}}の寺院（恵日山東光寺）の境内に再建され、明神社に改称する。 * {{jalink|1870年|Q7741}}（明治3年）、爾波神社に改称され、{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}（明治5年）に郷社になる。 * {{jalink|1945年|Q5240}}（昭和20年）7月28日、{{jalink|太平洋戦争|Q184425}}の{{jalink|空襲|Q2380335}}（{{jalink|一宮空襲|Q48744775}}）で社殿は全て焼失する。現在の社殿は{{jalink|1948年|Q5165}}（昭和23年）の再建である。 === 祭神 === * {{jalink|神八井耳命|Q11588756}} === 交通機関 === * {{jalink|名鉄バス|Q6810397}}「丹羽」バス停下車、徒歩10分 ** {{jalink|名古屋鉄道|Q30850}}{{jalink|名鉄名古屋本線|Q1137838|名古屋本線}}・{{jalink|名鉄尾西線|Q901005|尾西線}}{{jalink|名鉄一宮駅|Q2856589}}、{{jalink|東海道本線|Q1190152}}{{jalink|尾張一宮駅|Q871495}}（{{jalink|名鉄一宮駅バスターミナル}}3番のりば）より「宮田本郷（丹羽経由）」行き === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にわしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly (元号)|Q1192853|天文}}6年）に戦災で社殿焼失する。再建されるが、{{jalink|1666年|Q7585}}（{{jalink|寛文|Q1146228}}6年）に洪水で破損。現在地に移転する。このさい、{{jalink|臨済宗|Q639625}}の寺院（恵日山東光寺）の境内に再建され、明神社に改称する。 * {{jalink|1870年|Q7741}}（明治3年）、爾波神社に改称され、{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}（明治5年）に郷社になる。 * {{jalink|1945年|Q5240}}（昭和20年）7月28日、{{jalink|太平洋戦争|Q184425}}の{{jalink|空襲|Q2380335}}（{{jalink|一宮空襲|Q48744775}}）で社殿は全て焼失する。現在の社殿は{{jalink|1948年|Q5165}}（昭和23年）の再建である。 === 祭神 === * {{jalink|神八井耳命|Q11588756}} === 交通機関 === * {{jalink|名鉄バス|Q6810397}}「丹羽」バス停下車、徒歩10分 ** {{jalink|名古屋鉄道|Q30850}}{{jalink|名鉄名古屋本線|Q1137838|名古屋本線}}・{{jalink|名鉄尾西線|Q901005|尾西線}}{{jalink|名鉄一宮駅|Q2856589}}、{{jalink|東海道本線|Q1190152}}{{jalink|尾張一宮駅|Q871495}}（{{jalink|名鉄一宮駅バスターミナル}}3番のりば）より「宮田本郷（丹羽経由）」行き === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にわしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] link|Q11569496}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2014年10月27日 (月) 12:50 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称 = 爾波神社 |画像 = [[File:Niwa-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|250px]]
鳥居 |所在地 = {{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|一宮市|Q270810}}{{jalink|丹羽 (一宮市)|Q127432607|丹羽}}字宮浦1410 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|神八井耳命|Q11588756}} |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}・旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}} |創建 = 不明 |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=爾波神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''爾波神社'''（にわじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|一宮市|Q270810}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}。 {{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}の{{jalink|尾張国|Q514506}}{{jalink|丹羽郡|Q1347719}}「尓波神社」である。丹羽郡の総社と伝えられ、{{jalink|丹羽氏|Q2661652|丹羽臣}}に関係が深い。 === 概況 === * 創建時期は不明。一説では{{jalink|701年|Q23496}}（{{jalink|大宝 (日本)|Q1346269|大宝}}元年）創建という。この地域に勢力があった尾張丹羽臣が、祖先の神八井耳命を祀ったのが始まりという。 * 創建当初の名称は尓波神社。{{jalink|鎌倉時代|Q236205}}以降は「丹羽天神」「丹羽大神」「爾波天神」と呼ばれていた。 * {{jalink|1537年|Q6461}}（{{jalink|天文 (元号)|Q1192853|天文}}6年）に戦災で社殿焼失する。再建されるが、{{jalink|1666年|Q7585}}（{{jalink|寛文|Q1146228}}6年）に洪水で破損。現在地に移転する。このさい、{{jalink|臨済宗|Q639625}}の寺院（恵日山東光寺）の境内に再建され、明神社に改称する。 * {{jalink|1870年|Q7741}}（明治3年）、爾波神社に改称され、{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}（明治5年）に郷社になる。 * {{jalink|1945年|Q5240}}（昭和20年）7月28日、{{jalink|太平洋戦争|Q184425}}の{{jalink|空襲|Q2380335}}（{{jalink|一宮空襲|Q48744775}}）で社殿は全て焼失する。現在の社殿は{{jalink|1948年|Q5165}}（昭和23年）の再建である。 === 祭神 === * {{jalink|神八井耳命|Q11588756}} === 交通機関 === * {{jalink|名鉄バス|Q6810397}}「丹羽」バス停下車、徒歩10分 ** {{jalink|名古屋鉄道|Q30850}}{{jalink|名鉄名古屋本線|Q1137838|名古屋本線}}・{{jalink|名鉄尾西線|Q901005|尾西線}}{{jalink|名鉄一宮駅|Q2856589}}、{{jalink|東海道本線|Q1190152}}{{jalink|尾張一宮駅|Q871495}}（{{jalink|名鉄一宮駅バスターミナル}}3番のりば）より「宮田本郷（丹羽経由）」行き === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にわしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Niwa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Niwa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧郷社]] [[Category:need translation]]

link|Q11569496}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=October 27, 2014 (Mon) 12:50 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称 = Niwa Shrine |画像 = [[File:Niwa-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|250px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}{{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q270810}}{{jalink|Niwa (Ichinomiya)|Q127432607|Niwa}} Aza Miyaura 1410 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} / former {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}} |創建 = Unknown |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Niwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Niwa Shrine''' (にわじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q270810}}, {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}. Its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}. It is the “Niwa Shrine” of {{jalink|Niwa District|Q1347719}}, {{jalink|Owari Province|Q514506}}, listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Engishiki}}. It is said to have been the sōsha of Niwa District, and has deep ties to the {{jalink|Niwa clan|Q2661652|Niwa no Omi}}. === Overview === * The founding period is unknown. One theory says it was founded in {{jalink|701|Q23496}} ({{jalink|Taihō (Japan)|Q1346269|Taihō}} 1). It is said to have begun when the Owari Niwa no Omi, who held influence in this area, enshrined their ancestor Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto. * Its original name was Niwa Shrine. From the {{jalink|Kamakura period|Q236205}} onward, it was called “Niwa Tenjin,” “Niwa Ōkami,” or “Niwa Tenjin.” * In {{jalink|1537|Q6461}} ({{jalink|Tenbun (era)|Q1192853|Tenbun}} 6), the shrine buildings were destroyed by war fire. They were rebuilt, but in {{jalink|1666|Q7585}} ({{jalink|Kanbun|Q1146228}} 6) they were damaged by flood. The shrine was moved to its current site. At that time, it was rebuilt in the precincts of a {{jalink|Rinzai school|Q639625}} temple (Keijizan Tōkō-ji) and renamed Myōjin-sha. * In {{jalink|1870|Q7741}} (Meiji 3), it was renamed Niwa Shrine, and in {{jalink|1872|Q7753}} (Meiji 5), it became a gōsha. * On July 28, {{jalink|1945|Q5240}} (Shōwa 20), all shrine buildings were destroyed in an air raid ({{jalink|Ichinomiya Air Raid|Q48744775}}) during the {{jalink|Pacific War|Q184425}}. The current buildings were rebuilt in {{jalink|1948|Q5165}} (Shōwa 23). === Enshrined deity === * {{jalink|Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756}} === Access === * {{jalink|Meitetsu Bus|Q6810397}}: get off at “Niwa” bus stop, 10 minutes on foot ** From {{jalink|Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589}} on the {{jalink|Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838}}/{{jalink|Bisai Line|Q901005}} of {{jalink|Nagoya Railroad|Q30850}}, or from {{jalink|Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495}} on the {{jalink|Tōkaidō Main Line|Q1190152}} (Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station bus terminal, platform 3), take a bus bound for “Miyata Hongō (via Niwa).” === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にわしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Niwa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Niwa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧郷社]] [[Category:need translation]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * にわじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 6180005009930 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '愛知県一宮市丹羽字宮浦1410', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:爾波神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11569496}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=October 27, 2014 (Mon) 12:50 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称 = Niwa Shrine |画像 = [[File:Niwa-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|250px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}{{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q270810}}{{jalink|Niwa (Ichinomiya)|Q127432607|Niwa}} Aza Miyaura 1410 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} / former {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}} |創建 = Unknown |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Niwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Niwa Shrine''' (にわじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q270810}}, {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}. Its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}. It is the “Niwa Shrine” of {{jalink|Niwa District|Q1347719}}, {{jalink|Owari Province|Q514506}}, listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Engishiki}}. It is said to have been the sōsha of Niwa District, and has deep ties to the {{jalink|Niwa clan|Q2661652|Niwa no Omi}}. === Overview === * The founding period is unknown. One theory says it was founded in {{jalink|701|Q23496}} ({{jalink|Taihō (Japan)|Q1346269|Taihō}} 1). It is said to have begun when the Owari Niwa no Omi, who held influence in this area, enshrined their ancestor Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto. * Its original name was Niwa Shrine. From the {{jalink|Kamakura period|Q236205}} onward, it was called “Niwa Tenjin,” “Niwa Ōkami,” or “Niwa Tenjin.” * In {{jalink|1537|Q6461}} ({{jalink|Tenbun (era)|Q1192853|Tenbun}} 6), the shrine buildings were destroyed by war fire. They were rebuilt, but in {{jalink|1666|Q7585}} ({{jalink|Kanbun|Q1146228}} 6) they were damaged by flood. The shrine was moved to its current site. At that time, it was rebuilt in the precincts of a {{jalink|Rinzai school|Q639625}} temple (Keijizan Tōkō-ji) and renamed Myōjin-sha. * In {{jalink|1870|Q7741}} (Meiji 3), it was renamed Niwa Shrine, and in {{jalink|1872|Q7753}} (Meiji 5), it became a gōsha. * On July 28, {{jalink|1945|Q5240}} (Shōwa 20), all shrine buildings were destroyed in an air raid ({{jalink|Ichinomiya Air Raid|Q48744775}}) during the {{jalink|Pacific War|Q184425}}. The current buildings were rebuilt in {{jalink|1948|Q5165}} (Shōwa 23). === Enshrined deity === * {{jalink|Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756}} === Access === * {{jalink|Meitetsu Bus|Q6810397}}: get off at “Niwa” bus stop, 10 minutes on foot ** From {{jalink|Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589}} on the {{jalink|Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838}}/{{jalink|Bisai Line|Q901005}} of {{jalink|Nagoya Railroad|Q30850}}, or from {{jalink|Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495}} on the {{jalink|Tōkaidō Main Line|Q1190152}} (Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station bus terminal, platform 3), take a bus bound for “Miyata Hongō (via Niwa).” === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にわしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Niwa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Niwa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧郷社]] [[Category:need translation]]

It is located in [[Owari Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická Niwa-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Q11588756|lt=Q11588756|WD=Q11588756}} == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181395 ** P3831: 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|lt=Q3029647|WD=Q3029647}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|lt=Q17|WD=Q17}} == name in kana (P1814) == * にわじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 6180005009930 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '愛知県一宮市丹羽字宮浦1410', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:爾波神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11569496}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=October 27, 2014 (Mon) 12:50 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称 = Niwa Shrine |画像 = [[File:Niwa-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|250px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}{{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q270810}}{{jalink|Niwa (Ichinomiya)|Q127432607|Niwa}} Aza Miyaura 1410 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} / former {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}} |創建 = Unknown |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Niwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Niwa Shrine''' (にわじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q270810}}, {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}. Its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}. It is the “Niwa Shrine” of {{jalink|Niwa District|Q1347719}}, {{jalink|Owari Province|Q514506}}, listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Engishiki}}. It is said to have been the sōsha of Niwa District, and has deep ties to the {{jalink|Niwa clan|Q2661652|Niwa no Omi}}. === Overview === * The founding period is unknown. One theory says it was founded in {{jalink|701|Q23496}} ({{jalink|Taihō (Japan)|Q1346269|Taihō}} 1). It is said to have begun when the Owari Niwa no Omi, who held influence in this area, enshrined their ancestor Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto. * Its original name was Niwa Shrine. From the {{jalink|Kamakura period|Q236205}} onward, it was called “Niwa Tenjin,” “Niwa Ōkami,” or “Niwa Tenjin.” * In {{jalink|1537|Q6461}} ({{jalink|Tenbun (era)|Q1192853|Tenbun}} 6), the shrine buildings were destroyed by war fire. They were rebuilt, but in {{jalink|1666|Q7585}} ({{jalink|Kanbun|Q1146228}} 6) they were damaged by flood. The shrine was moved to its current site. At that time, it was rebuilt in the precincts of a {{jalink|Rinzai school|Q639625}} temple (Keijizan Tōkō-ji) and renamed Myōjin-sha. * In {{jalink|1870|Q7741}} (Meiji 3), it was renamed Niwa Shrine, and in {{jalink|1872|Q7753}} (Meiji 5), it became a gōsha. * On July 28, {{jalink|1945|Q5240}} (Shōwa 20), all shrine buildings were destroyed in an air raid ({{jalink|Ichinomiya Air Raid|Q48744775}}) during the {{jalink|Pacific War|Q184425}}. The current buildings were rebuilt in {{jalink|1948|Q5165}} (Shōwa 23). === Enshrined deity === * {{jalink|Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756}} === Access === * {{jalink|Meitetsu Bus|Q6810397}}: get off at “Niwa” bus stop, 10 minutes on foot ** From {{jalink|Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589}} on the {{jalink|Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838}}/{{jalink|Bisai Line|Q901005}} of {{jalink|Nagoya Railroad|Q30850}}, or from {{jalink|Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495}} on the {{jalink|Tōkaidō Main Line|Q1190152}} (Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station bus terminal, platform 3), take a bus bound for “Miyata Hongō (via Niwa).” === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にわしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Niwa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Niwa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧郷社]] [[Category:need translation]]

Torii |所在地 = {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}{{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q270810}}{{jalink|Niwa (Ichinomiya)|Q127432607|Niwa}} Aza Miyaura 1410 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} / former {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}} |創建 = Unknown |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Niwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Niwa Shrine''' (にわじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q270810}}, {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}. Its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}. It is the “Niwa Shrine” of {{jalink|Niwa District|Q1347719}}, {{jalink|Owari Province|Q514506}}, listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Engishiki}}. It is said to have been the sōsha of Niwa District, and has deep ties to the {{jalink|Niwa clan|Q2661652|Niwa no Omi}}. === Overview === * The founding period is unknown. One theory says it was founded in {{jalink|701|Q23496}} ({{jalink|Taihō (Japan)|Q1346269|Taihō}} 1). It is said to have begun when the Owari Niwa no Omi, who held influence in this area, enshrined their ancestor Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto. * Its original name was Niwa Shrine. From the {{jalink|Kamakura period|Q236205}} onward, it was called “Niwa Tenjin,” “Niwa Ōkami,” or “Niwa Tenjin.” * In {{jalink|1537|Q6461}} ({{jalink|Tenbun (era)|Q1192853|Tenbun}} 6), the shrine buildings were destroyed by war fire. They were rebuilt, but in {{jalink|1666|Q7585}} ({{jalink|Kanbun|Q1146228}} 6) they were damaged by flood. The shrine was moved to its current site. At that time, it was rebuilt in the precincts of a {{jalink|Rinzai school|Q639625}} temple (Keijizan Tōkō-ji) and renamed Myōjin-sha. * In {{jalink|1870|Q7741}} (Meiji 3), it was renamed Niwa Shrine, and in {{jalink|1872|Q7753}} (Meiji 5), it became a gōsha. * On July 28, {{jalink|1945|Q5240}} (Shōwa 20), all shrine buildings were destroyed in an air raid ({{jalink|Ichinomiya Air Raid|Q48744775}}) during the {{jalink|Pacific War|Q184425}}. The current buildings were rebuilt in {{jalink|1948|Q5165}} (Shōwa 23). === Enshrined deity === * {{jalink|Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756}} === Access === * {{jalink|Meitetsu Bus|Q6810397}}: get off at “Niwa” bus stop, 10 minutes on foot ** From {{jalink|Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589}} on the {{jalink|Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838}}/{{jalink|Bisai Line|Q901005}} of {{jalink|Nagoya Railroad|Q30850}}, or from {{jalink|Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495}} on the {{jalink|Tōkaidō Main Line|Q1190152}} (Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station bus terminal, platform 3), take a bus bound for “Miyata Hongō (via Niwa).” === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にわしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Niwa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Niwa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧郷社]]

link|Q11569496}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=October 27, 2014 (Mon) 12:50 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称 = Niwa Shrine |画像 = [[File:Niwa-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|250px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810}}{{ill|lt=Niwa (Ichinomiya)|Q127432607|WD=Q127432607|lt=Niwa}} Aza Miyaura 1410 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756}} |社格 = {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} / former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |創建 = Unknown |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Niwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Niwa Shrine''' (にわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. It is the “Niwa Shrine” of {{ill|lt=Niwa District|Q1347719|WD=Q1347719}}, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki}}. It is said to have been the sōsha of Niwa District, and has deep ties to the {{ill|lt=Niwa clan|Q2661652|WD=Q2661652|lt=Niwa no Omi}}. === Overview === * The founding period is unknown. One theory says it was founded in {{ill|lt=701|Q23496|WD=Q23496}} ({{ill|lt=Taihō (Japan)|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō}} 1). It is said to have begun when the Owari Niwa no Omi, who held influence in this area, enshrined their ancestor Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto. * Its original name was Niwa Shrine. From the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205}} onward, it was called “Niwa Tenjin,” “Niwa Ōkami,” or “Niwa Tenjin.” * In {{ill|lt=1537|Q6461|WD=Q6461}} ({{ill|lt=Tenbun (era)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun}} 6), the shrine buildings were destroyed by war fire. They were rebuilt, but in {{ill|lt=1666|Q7585|WD=Q7585}} ({{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} 6) they were damaged by flood. The shrine was moved to its current site. At that time, it was rebuilt in the precincts of a {{ill|lt=Rinzai school|Q639625|WD=Q639625}} temple (Keijizan Tōkō-ji) and renamed Myōjin-sha. * In {{ill|lt=1870|Q7741|WD=Q7741}} (Meiji 3), it was renamed Niwa Shrine, and in {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}} (Meiji 5), it became a gōsha. * On July 28, {{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240}} (Shōwa 20), all shrine buildings were destroyed in an air raid ({{ill|lt=Ichinomiya Air Raid|Q48744775|WD=Q48744775}}) during the {{ill|lt=Pacific War|Q184425|WD=Q184425}}. The current buildings were rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1948|Q5165|WD=Q5165}} (Shōwa 23). === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756}} === Access === * {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bus|Q6810397|WD=Q6810397}}: get off at “Niwa” bus stop, 10 minutes on foot ** From {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589|WD=Q2856589}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838}}/{{ill|lt=Bisai Line|Q901005|WD=Q901005}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850}}, or from {{ill|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495|WD=Q871495}} on the {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line|Q1190152|WD=Q1190152}} (Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station bus terminal, platform 3), take a bus bound for “Miyata Hongō (via Niwa).” === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にわしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Niwa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Niwa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧郷社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

link|Q11569496}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=October 27, 2014 (Mon) 12:50 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称 = Niwa Shrine |画像 = [[File:Niwa-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|250px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810}}{{ill|lt=Niwa (Ichinomiya)|Q127432607|WD=Q127432607|lt=Niwa}} Aza Miyaura 1410 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} / former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |創建 = Unknown |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Niwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Niwa Shrine''' (にわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. It is the “Niwa Shrine” of {{ill|lt=Niwa District|Q1347719|WD=Q1347719}}, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki}}. It is said to have been the sōsha of Niwa District, and has deep ties to the {{ill|lt=Niwa clan|Q2661652|WD=Q2661652|lt=Niwa no Omi}}. === Overview === * The founding period is unknown. One theory says it was founded in {{ill|lt=701|Q23496|WD=Q23496}} ({{ill|lt=Taihō (Japan)|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō}} 1). It is said to have begun when the Owari Niwa no Omi, who held influence in this area, enshrined their ancestor Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto. * Its original name was Niwa Shrine. From the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205}} onward, it was called “Niwa Tenjin,” “Niwa Ōkami,” or “Niwa Tenjin.” * In {{ill|lt=1537|Q6461|WD=Q6461}} ({{ill|lt=Tenbun (era)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun}} 6), the shrine buildings were destroyed by war fire. They were rebuilt, but in {{ill|lt=1666|Q7585|WD=Q7585}} ({{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} 6) they were damaged by flood. The shrine was moved to its current site. At that time, it was rebuilt in the precincts of a {{ill|lt=Rinzai school|Q639625|WD=Q639625}} temple (Keijizan Tōkō-ji) and renamed Myōjin-sha. * In {{ill|lt=1870|Q7741|WD=Q7741}} (Meiji 3), it was renamed Niwa Shrine, and in {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}} (Meiji 5), it became a gōsha. * On July 28, {{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240}} (Shōwa 20), all shrine buildings were destroyed in an air raid ({{ill|lt=Ichinomiya Air Raid|Q48744775|WD=Q48744775}}) during the {{ill|lt=Pacific War|Q184425|WD=Q184425}}. The current buildings were rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1948|Q5165|WD=Q5165}} (Shōwa 23). === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756}} === Access === * {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bus|Q6810397|WD=Q6810397}}: get off at “Niwa” bus stop, 10 minutes on foot ** From {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589|WD=Q2856589}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838}}/{{ill|lt=Bisai Line|Q901005|WD=Q901005}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850}}, or from {{ill|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495|WD=Q871495}} on the {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line|Q1190152|WD=Q1190152}} (Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station bus terminal, platform 3), take a bus bound for “Miyata Hongō (via Niwa).” === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にわしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Niwa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Niwa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧郷社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

At that time, it was rebuilt in the precincts of a {{ill|lt=Rinzai school|Q639625|WD=Q639625}} temple (Keijizan Tōkō-ji) and renamed Myōjin-sha. * In {{ill|lt=1870|Q7741|WD=Q7741}} (Meiji 3), it was renamed Niwa Shrine, and in {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}} (Meiji 5), it became a gōsha. * On July 28, {{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240}} (Shōwa 20), all shrine buildings were destroyed in an air raid ({{ill|lt=Ichinomiya Air Raid|Q48744775|WD=Q48744775}}) during the {{ill|lt=Pacific War|Q184425|WD=Q184425}}. The current buildings were rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1948|Q5165|WD=Q5165}} (Shōwa 23). === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756}} === Access === * {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bus|Q6810397|WD=Q6810397}}: get off at “Niwa” bus stop, 10 minutes on foot ** From {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589|WD=Q2856589}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838}}/{{ill|lt=Bisai Line|Q901005|WD=Q901005}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850}}, or from {{ill|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495|WD=Q871495}} on the {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line|Q1190152|WD=Q1190152}} (Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station bus terminal, platform 3), take a bus bound for “Miyata Hongō (via Niwa).” === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にわしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Niwa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Niwa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧郷社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

== {{出典の明記|date=October 27, 2014 (Mon) 12:50 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称 = Niwa Shrine |画像 = [[File:Niwa-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|250px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}{{ill|lt=Niwa (Ichinomiya)|Q127432607|WD=Q127432607|lt=Niwa|1=Niwa}} Aza Miyaura 1410 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} / former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Niwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Niwa Shrine''' (にわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is the “Niwa Shrine” of {{ill|lt=Niwa District|Q1347719|WD=Q1347719|1=Niwa District}}, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. It is said to have been the sōsha of Niwa District, and has deep ties to the {{ill|lt=Niwa clan|Q2661652|WD=Q2661652|lt=Niwa no Omi|1=Niwa clan}}. === Overview === * The founding period is unknown. One theory says it was founded in {{ill|lt=701|Q23496|WD=Q23496|1=701}} ({{ill|lt=Taihō (Japan)|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō|1=Taihō (era)}} 1). It is said to have begun when the Owari Niwa no Omi, who held influence in this area, enshrined their ancestor Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto. * Its original name was Niwa Shrine. From the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}} onward, it was called “Niwa Tenjin,” “Niwa Ōkami,” or “Niwa Tenjin.” * In {{ill|lt=1537|Q6461|WD=Q6461|1=1537}} ({{ill|lt=Tenbun (era)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun|1=Tenbun}} 6), the shrine buildings were destroyed by war fire. They were rebuilt, but in {{ill|lt=1666|Q7585|WD=Q7585|1=1666}} ({{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 6) they were damaged by flood. The shrine was moved to its current site. At that time, it was rebuilt in the precincts of a {{ill|lt=Rinzai school|Q639625|WD=Q639625|1=Rinzai school}} temple (Keijizan Tōkō-ji) and renamed Myōjin-sha. * In {{ill|lt=1870|Q7741|WD=Q7741|1=1870}} (Meiji 3), it was renamed Niwa Shrine, and in {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5), it became a gōsha. * On July 28, {{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240|1=1945}} (Shōwa 20), all shrine buildings were destroyed in an air raid ({{ill|lt=Ichinomiya Air Raid|Q48744775|WD=Q48744775|1=Bombing of Ichinomiya}}) during the {{ill|lt=Pacific War|Q184425|WD=Q184425|1=Pacific War}}. The current buildings were rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1948|Q5165|WD=Q5165|1=1948}} (Shōwa 23). === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}} === Access === * {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bus|Q6810397|WD=Q6810397|1=Meitetsu Bus}}: get off at “Niwa” bus stop, 10 minutes on foot ** From {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589|WD=Q2856589|1=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838|1=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}}/{{ill|lt=Bisai Line|Q901005|WD=Q901005|1=Meitetsu Bisai Line}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|1=Meitetsu}}, or from {{ill|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495|WD=Q871495|1=Owari-Ichinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line|Q1190152|WD=Q1190152|1=Tōkaidō Main Line}} (Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station bus terminal, platform 3), take a bus bound for “Miyata Hongō (via Niwa).” === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にわしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Niwa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Niwa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧郷社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

The current buildings were rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1948|Q5165|WD=Q5165|1=1948}} (Shōwa 23). === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}} === Access === * {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bus|Q6810397|WD=Q6810397|1=Meitetsu Bus}}: get off at “Niwa” bus stop, 10 minutes on foot ** From {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589|WD=Q2856589|1=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838|1=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}}/{{ill|lt=Bisai Line|Q901005|WD=Q901005|1=Meitetsu Bisai Line}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|1=Meitetsu}}, or from {{ill|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495|WD=Q871495|1=Owari-Ichinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line|Q1190152|WD=Q1190152|1=Tōkaidō Main Line}} (Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station bus terminal, platform 3), take a bus bound for “Miyata Hongō (via Niwa).” === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にわしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Niwa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Niwa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧郷社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

== {{出典の明記|date=October 27, 2014 (Mon) 12:50 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称 = Niwa Shrine |画像 = [[File:Niwa-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|250px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}{{ill|lt=Niwa (Ichinomiya)|Q127432607|WD=Q127432607|lt=Niwa|1=Niwa}} Aza Miyaura 1410 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} / former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Niwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Niwa Shrine''' (にわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is the “Niwa Shrine” of {{ill|lt=Niwa District|Q1347719|WD=Q1347719|1=Niwa District}}, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. It is said to have been the sōsha of Niwa District, and has deep ties to the {{ill|lt=Niwa clan|Q2661652|WD=Q2661652|lt=Niwa no Omi|1=Niwa clan}}. === Overview === * The founding period is unknown. One theory says it was founded in {{ill|lt=701|Q23496|WD=Q23496|1=701}} ({{ill|lt=Taihō (Japan)|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō|1=Taihō (era)}} 1). It is said to have begun when the Owari Niwa no Omi, who held influence in this area, enshrined their ancestor Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto. * Its original name was Niwa Shrine. From the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}} onward, it was called “Niwa Tenjin,” “Niwa Ōkami,” or “Niwa Tenjin.” * In {{ill|lt=1537|Q6461|WD=Q6461|1=1537}} ({{ill|lt=Tenbun (era)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun|1=Tenbun}} 6), the shrine buildings were destroyed by war fire. They were rebuilt, but in {{ill|lt=1666|Q7585|WD=Q7585|1=1666}} ({{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 6) they were damaged by flood. The shrine was moved to its current site. At that time, it was rebuilt in the precincts of a {{ill|lt=Rinzai school|Q639625|WD=Q639625|1=Rinzai school}} temple (Keijizan Tōkō-ji) and renamed Myōjin-sha. * In {{ill|lt=1870|Q7741|WD=Q7741|1=1870}} (Meiji 3), it was renamed Niwa Shrine, and in {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5), it became a gōsha. * On July 28, {{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240|1=1945}} (Shōwa 20), all shrine buildings were destroyed in an air raid ({{ill|lt=Ichinomiya Air Raid|Q48744775|WD=Q48744775|1=Bombing of Ichinomiya}}) during the {{ill|lt=Pacific War|Q184425|WD=Q184425|1=Pacific War}}. The current buildings were rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1948|Q5165|WD=Q5165|1=1948}} (Shōwa 23). === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}} === Access === * {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bus|Q6810397|WD=Q6810397|1=Meitetsu Bus}}: get off at “Niwa” bus stop, 10 minutes on foot ** From {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589|WD=Q2856589|1=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838|1=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}}/{{ill|lt=Bisai Line|Q901005|WD=Q901005|1=Meitetsu Bisai Line}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|1=Meitetsu}}, or from {{ill|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495|WD=Q871495|1=Owari-Ichinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line|Q1190152|WD=Q1190152|1=Tōkaidō Main Line}} (Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station bus terminal, platform 3), take a bus bound for “Miyata Hongō (via Niwa).” === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にわしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Niwa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Niwa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧郷社]][[Category:qq]]

The current buildings were rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1948|Q5165|WD=Q5165|1=1948}} (Shōwa 23). === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}} === Access === * {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bus|Q6810397|WD=Q6810397|1=Meitetsu Bus}}: get off at “Niwa” bus stop, 10 minutes on foot ** From {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589|WD=Q2856589|1=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838|1=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}}/{{ill|lt=Bisai Line|Q901005|WD=Q901005|1=Meitetsu Bisai Line}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|1=Meitetsu}}, or from {{ill|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495|WD=Q871495|1=Owari-Ichinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line|Q1190152|WD=Q1190152|1=Tōkaidō Main Line}} (Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station bus terminal, platform 3), take a bus bound for “Miyata Hongō (via Niwa).” === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Niwa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Niwa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧郷社]][[Category:qq]]

== {{出典の明記|date=October 27, 2014 (Mon) 12:50 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称 = Niwa Shrine |画像 = [[File:Niwa-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|250px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}{{ill|lt=Niwa (Ichinomiya)|Q127432607|WD=Q127432607|lt=Niwa|1=Niwa}} Aza Miyaura 1410 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} / former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Niwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Niwa Shrine''' (にわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is the “Niwa Shrine” of {{ill|lt=Niwa District|Q1347719|WD=Q1347719|1=Niwa District}}, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. It is said to have been the sōsha of Niwa District, and has deep ties to the {{ill|lt=Niwa clan|Q2661652|WD=Q2661652|lt=Niwa no Omi|1=Niwa clan}}. === Overview === * The founding period is unknown. One theory says it was founded in {{ill|lt=701|Q23496|WD=Q23496|1=701}} ({{ill|lt=Taihō (Japan)|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō|1=Taihō (era)}} 1). It is said to have begun when the Owari Niwa no Omi, who held influence in this area, enshrined their ancestor Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto. * Its original name was Niwa Shrine. From the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}} onward, it was called “Niwa Tenjin,” “Niwa Ōkami,” or “Niwa Tenjin.” * In {{ill|lt=1537|Q6461|WD=Q6461|1=1537}} ({{ill|lt=Tenbun (era)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun|1=Tenbun}} 6), the shrine buildings were destroyed by war fire. They were rebuilt, but in {{ill|lt=1666|Q7585|WD=Q7585|1=1666}} ({{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 6) they were damaged by flood. The shrine was moved to its current site. At that time, it was rebuilt in the precincts of a {{ill|lt=Rinzai school|Q639625|WD=Q639625|1=Rinzai school}} temple (Keijizan Tōkō-ji) and renamed Myōjin-sha. * In {{ill|lt=1870|Q7741|WD=Q7741|1=1870}} (Meiji 3), it was renamed Niwa Shrine, and in {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5), it became a gōsha. * On July 28, {{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240|1=1945}} (Shōwa 20), all shrine buildings were destroyed in an air raid ({{ill|lt=Ichinomiya Air Raid|Q48744775|WD=Q48744775|1=Bombing of Ichinomiya}}) during the {{ill|lt=Pacific War|Q184425|WD=Q184425|1=Pacific War}}. The current buildings were rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1948|Q5165|WD=Q5165|1=1948}} (Shōwa 23). === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}} === Access === * {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bus|Q6810397|WD=Q6810397|1=Meitetsu Bus}}: get off at “Niwa” bus stop, 10 minutes on foot ** From {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589|WD=Q2856589|1=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838|1=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}}/{{ill|lt=Bisai Line|Q901005|WD=Q901005|1=Meitetsu Bisai Line}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|1=Meitetsu}}, or from {{ill|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495|WD=Q871495|1=Owari-Ichinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line|Q1190152|WD=Q1190152|1=Tōkaidō Main Line}} (Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station bus terminal, platform 3), take a bus bound for “Miyata Hongō (via Niwa).” === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Niwa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Niwa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ichinomiya, Aichi]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧郷社]][[Category:qq]]

The current buildings were rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1948|Q5165|WD=Q5165|1=1948}} (Shōwa 23). === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}} === Access === * {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bus|Q6810397|WD=Q6810397|1=Meitetsu Bus}}: get off at “Niwa” bus stop, 10 minutes on foot ** From {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589|WD=Q2856589|1=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838|1=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}}/{{ill|lt=Bisai Line|Q901005|WD=Q901005|1=Meitetsu Bisai Line}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|1=Meitetsu}}, or from {{ill|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495|WD=Q871495|1=Owari-Ichinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line|Q1190152|WD=Q1190152|1=Tōkaidō Main Line}} (Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station bus terminal, platform 3), take a bus bound for “Miyata Hongō (via Niwa).” === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Niwa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Niwa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ichinomiya, Aichi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:愛知県の旧郷社]][[Category:qq]]

== {{出典の明記|date=October 27, 2014 (Mon) 12:50 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称 = Niwa Shrine |画像 = [[File:Niwa-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|250px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}{{ill|lt=Niwa (Ichinomiya)|Q127432607|WD=Q127432607|lt=Niwa|1=Niwa}} Aza Miyaura 1410 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} / former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Niwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Niwa Shrine''' (にわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is the “Niwa Shrine” of {{ill|lt=Niwa District|Q1347719|WD=Q1347719|1=Niwa District}}, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. It is said to have been the sōsha of Niwa District, and has deep ties to the {{ill|lt=Niwa clan|Q2661652|WD=Q2661652|lt=Niwa no Omi|1=Niwa clan}}. === Overview === * The founding period is unknown. One theory says it was founded in {{ill|lt=701|Q23496|WD=Q23496|1=701}} ({{ill|lt=Taihō (Japan)|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō|1=Taihō (era)}} 1). It is said to have begun when the Owari Niwa no Omi, who held influence in this area, enshrined their ancestor Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto. * Its original name was Niwa Shrine. From the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}} onward, it was called “Niwa Tenjin,” “Niwa Ōkami,” or “Niwa Tenjin.” * In {{ill|lt=1537|Q6461|WD=Q6461|1=1537}} ({{ill|lt=Tenbun (era)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun|1=Tenbun}} 6), the shrine buildings were destroyed by war fire. They were rebuilt, but in {{ill|lt=1666|Q7585|WD=Q7585|1=1666}} ({{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 6) they were damaged by flood. The shrine was moved to its current site. At that time, it was rebuilt in the precincts of a {{ill|lt=Rinzai school|Q639625|WD=Q639625|1=Rinzai school}} temple (Keijizan Tōkō-ji) and renamed Myōjin-sha. * In {{ill|lt=1870|Q7741|WD=Q7741|1=1870}} (Meiji 3), it was renamed Niwa Shrine, and in {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5), it became a gōsha. * On July 28, {{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240|1=1945}} (Shōwa 20), all shrine buildings were destroyed in an air raid ({{ill|lt=Ichinomiya Air Raid|Q48744775|WD=Q48744775|1=Bombing of Ichinomiya}}) during the {{ill|lt=Pacific War|Q184425|WD=Q184425|1=Pacific War}}. The current buildings were rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1948|Q5165|WD=Q5165|1=1948}} (Shōwa 23). === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}} === Access === * {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bus|Q6810397|WD=Q6810397|1=Meitetsu Bus}}: get off at “Niwa” bus stop, 10 minutes on foot ** From {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589|WD=Q2856589|1=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838|1=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}}/{{ill|lt=Bisai Line|Q901005|WD=Q901005|1=Meitetsu Bisai Line}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|1=Meitetsu}}, or from {{ill|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495|WD=Q871495|1=Owari-Ichinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line|Q1190152|WD=Q1190152|1=Tōkaidō Main Line}} (Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station bus terminal, platform 3), take a bus bound for “Miyata Hongō (via Niwa).” === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Niwa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Niwa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ichinomiya, Aichi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Aichi Prefecture]][[Category:qq]]

The current buildings were rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1948|Q5165|WD=Q5165|1=1948}} (Shōwa 23). === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}} === Access === * {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bus|Q6810397|WD=Q6810397|1=Meitetsu Bus}}: get off at “Niwa” bus stop, 10 minutes on foot ** From {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589|WD=Q2856589|1=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838|1=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}}/{{ill|lt=Bisai Line|Q901005|WD=Q901005|1=Meitetsu Bisai Line}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|1=Meitetsu}}, or from {{ill|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495|WD=Q871495|1=Owari-Ichinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line|Q1190152|WD=Q1190152|1=Tōkaidō Main Line}} (Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station bus terminal, platform 3), take a bus bound for “Miyata Hongō (via Niwa).” === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Niwa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Niwa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ichinomiya, Aichi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Aichi Prefecture]]

== {{出典の明記|date=October 27, 2014 (Mon) 12:50 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称 = Niwa Shrine |画像 = [[File:Niwa-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|250px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}{{ill|lt=Niwa (Ichinomiya)|Q127432607|WD=Q127432607|lt=Niwa|1=Niwa}} Aza Miyaura 1410 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} / former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Niwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Niwa Shrine''' (にわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is the “Niwa Shrine” of {{ill|lt=Niwa District|Q1347719|WD=Q1347719|1=Niwa District}}, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. It is said to have been the sōsha of Niwa District, and has deep ties to the {{ill|lt=Niwa clan|Q2661652|WD=Q2661652|lt=Niwa no Omi|1=Niwa clan}}. === Overview === * The founding period is unknown. One theory says it was founded in {{ill|lt=701|Q23496|WD=Q23496|1=701}} ({{ill|lt=Taihō (Japan)|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō|1=Taihō (era)}} 1). It is said to have begun when the Owari Niwa no Omi, who held influence in this area, enshrined their ancestor Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto. * Its original name was Niwa Shrine. From the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}} onward, it was called “Niwa Tenjin,” “Niwa Ōkami,” or “Niwa Tenjin.” * In {{ill|lt=1537|Q6461|WD=Q6461|1=1537}} ({{ill|lt=Tenbun (era)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun|1=Tenbun}} 6), the shrine buildings were destroyed by war fire. They were rebuilt, but in {{ill|lt=1666|Q7585|WD=Q7585|1=1666}} ({{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 6) they were damaged by flood. The shrine was moved to its current site. At that time, it was rebuilt in the precincts of a {{ill|lt=Rinzai school|Q639625|WD=Q639625|1=Rinzai school}} temple (Keijizan Tōkō-ji) and renamed Myōjin-sha. * In {{ill|lt=1870|Q7741|WD=Q7741|1=1870}} (Meiji 3), it was renamed Niwa Shrine, and in {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5), it became a gōsha. * On July 28, {{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240|1=1945}} (Shōwa 20), all shrine buildings were destroyed in an air raid ({{ill|lt=Ichinomiya Air Raid|Q48744775|WD=Q48744775|1=Bombing of Ichinomiya}}) during the {{ill|lt=Pacific War|Q184425|WD=Q184425|1=Pacific War}}. The current buildings were rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1948|Q5165|WD=Q5165|1=1948}} (Shōwa 23). === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}} === Access === * {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bus|Q6810397|WD=Q6810397|1=Meitetsu Bus}}: get off at “Niwa” bus stop, 10 minutes on foot ** From {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589|WD=Q2856589|1=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838|1=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}}/{{ill|lt=Bisai Line|Q901005|WD=Q901005|1=Meitetsu Bisai Line}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|1=Meitetsu}}, or from {{ill|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495|WD=Q871495|1=Owari-Ichinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line|Q1190152|WD=Q1190152|1=Tōkaidō Main Line}} (Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station bus terminal, platform 3), take a bus bound for “Miyata Hongō (via Niwa).” === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Niwa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Niwa Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ichinomiya, Aichi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Aichi Prefecture]]

They were rebuilt, but in {{ill|lt=1666|Q7585|WD=Q7585|1=1666}} ({{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 6) they were damaged by flood. The shrine was moved to its current site. At that time, it was rebuilt in the precincts of a {{ill|lt=Rinzai school|Q639625|WD=Q639625|1=Rinzai school}} temple (Keijizan Tōkō-ji) and renamed Myōjin-sha. * In {{ill|lt=1870|Q7741|WD=Q7741|1=1870}} (Meiji 3), it was renamed Niwa Shrine, and in {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5), it became a gōsha. * On July 28, {{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240|1=1945}} (Shōwa 20), all shrine buildings were destroyed in an air raid ({{ill|lt=Ichinomiya Air Raid|Q48744775|WD=Q48744775|1=Bombing of Ichinomiya}}) during the {{ill|lt=Pacific War|Q184425|WD=Q184425|1=Pacific War}}. The current buildings were rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1948|Q5165|WD=Q5165|1=1948}} (Shōwa 23). === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}} === Access === * {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bus|Q6810397|WD=Q6810397|1=Meitetsu Bus}}: get off at “Niwa” bus stop, 10 minutes on foot ** From {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589|WD=Q2856589|1=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838|1=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}}/{{ill|lt=Bisai Line|Q901005|WD=Q901005|1=Meitetsu Bisai Line}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|1=Meitetsu}}, or from {{ill|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495|WD=Q871495|1=Owari-Ichinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line|Q1190152|WD=Q1190152|1=Tōkaidō Main Line}} (Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station bus terminal, platform 3), take a bus bound for “Miyata Hongō (via Niwa).” === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Niwa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Niwa Shrine]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ichinomiya, Aichi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Aichi Prefecture]]
